specific_location: Along Arkansas River, west of Cottonwood Picnic Area, below Pueblo Reservoir dam.
description: I was birding alone along the Arkansas River, below Pueblo Reservoir dam.  I was walking west, from Cottonwood Picnic Area, along the south side of the Arkansas River, when I noticed a swift flying around with swallows (many Violet-green Swallows, among others).  The flight pattern of this swift, seemed off for Chimney Swift.  The bird had a very white throat, and paler rump, and short tail.  The wing span seemed shorter than nearby Violet-green Swallow.  The bird didn't make any noise, like Chimney Swifts usuallly do.  See photos.  They show the white throat, paler rump, short tail, short wings.
photographer: Photographer: Brandon K. Percival, Brandon K. Percival, Brandon K. Percival, Brandon K. Percival, Brandon K. Percival
